steps.title: Frame the lying puzzle
steps.content: The text begins with the idea that lying may be permissible in some circumstances, then asks what still grounds opposition to lying. Venkataraman's appeal to agential interests is introduced as one answer to that puzzle.

steps.title: Match agency and exceptions
steps.content: A contradicts the passage by making truthfulness inviolable. B says the grounds remain unclear, and D turns the argument into a practical method for identifying cases. The main idea is that respect for agency supports truthfulness, with exceptions in certain circumstances. The answer is C.
